response:
## Split plan for `src/billing.ts`

Evidence base from `crimes context`: 310 lines, 5 top-level exports, threshold 300 (`crime_00016`); the bulk lives in `generateInvoice` L50–253 (204 lines, 3.4× the 60-line domain-function threshold, `crime_00002`); 7 direct clock reads at L57/63/92/198/238/250/273 (`crime_00009`); misleading-name `calculateRefundPreview` L40–44 with `saveRefundAudit`/`sendRefundEmail` (`crime_00013`); prose rule at L39 not encoded in code (`crime_00017`); 4 self-flagged TODO/FIXME/HACK/XXX inside `generateInvoice` (`crime_00035`); "enterprise" literal duplicated across 4 files including this one (`crime_00012`).

The author already drew the seams — every region in `generateInvoice` starts with a `// -- … --` banner. The split follows those banners, not "feels too long."

### Chunks → new files

| New file | Source lines | What moves | Evidence tie |
|---|---|---|---|
| `src/billing/types.ts` | L4–31 | `User`, `InvoiceLine`, `Invoice` | Shared by every chunk below; prerequisite for the rest |
| `src/billing/tax.ts` | L145–157 **+** L257–268 | Unify both copies into one `tax(country, cents)` | `crime_00002` evidence "5 top-level functions" + author comment L255–256 admits the duplicate |
| `src/billing/currency.ts` | L159–165 | `currencyForCountry(country)` | Pure switch, separable |
| `src/billing/pricing.ts` | L66–126 | `applyPlanPricing`, `calculateLoyaltyDiscount(user, subtotal, now)`, `applyPromo` | 60 of the 204 God-Function lines; `now` injected closes L92 from `crime_00009` |
| `src/billing/fraud.ts` | L128–143 | `scoreFraudRisk(...)` | Author already wrote "does not belong in invoice generation" at L128 |
| `src/billing/audit.ts` | L182–199 | `auditInvoice` — drop PII | Resolves the L183 FIXME in `crime_00035` |
| `src/billing/metrics.ts` | L201–211 | `emitInvoiceMetrics` | L201 comment "should be a background job" |
| `src/billing/email.ts` | L213–250 | `composeInvoiceEmail` + `sendInvoiceEmail` with a real retry | L213 "mixes presentation with billing logic"; L236 HACK + L246 XXX in `crime_00035` |
| `src/billing/refund.ts` | L33–44 | `canRefund(user, plan, approval)` guard + `previewRefund` (pure) + `processRefund` (side-effecting) | `crime_00013` (False Identity) + `crime_00017` encoded as the guard |
| `src/billing/upgrade.ts` | L270–305 | `recommendUpgrade(user, now)` | Pure once `now` is injected (closes L273 in `crime_00009`) |
| `src/billing.ts` (slimmed) | — | Barrel: re-exports + `generateInvoice` reduced to orchestration | Keeps the public surface stable for any future import |

`listRecentInvoices` (L307–310) is a stub returning `[]` with a TODO — delete it in this pass or open a ticket; don't carry dead exports through the split.

### Order

1. **`types.ts`** first — zero behaviour change, unblocks everything else. Commit alone.
2. **`tax.ts`** — collapses one of the two duplicates `crime_00002` calls out; existing `tax()` export already has a public surface, so the unification is invisible to callers.
3. **`currency.ts`** — smallest pure extraction, builds confidence.
4. **`pricing.ts`** — biggest single win on the God Function (~60 lines out of 204). Take `now: () => number` as a parameter so loyalty math becomes testable.
5. **`fraud.ts`**, **`audit.ts`**, **`metrics.ts`**, **`email.ts`** — side-effecting chunks, one commit each. After this, `generateInvoice` is a ~30-line composition of named helpers and should drop below the 60-line threshold, clearing `crime_00002`.
6. **`refund.ts`** — encode the L39 rule as `canRefund`, rename `calculateRefundPreview` → split into `previewRefund` (pure, returns cents) and `processRefund` (audits + emails). This clears `crime_00013` and `crime_00017` together.
7. **`upgrade.ts`** — last; mostly a move, but inject `now` so all clock reads in this file are gone (closes `crime_00009`).
8. Re-run `crimes scan src/` and confirm `crime_00002`, `crime_00009`, `crime_00013`, `crime_00016`, `crime_00017`, `crime_00035` all clear.

### Callers to update

- **Inside the repo:** `rg "from ['\"].*billing"` finds zero importers — `src/billing.ts` is currently a leaf. `src/billing.test.ts` defines its own stubs and doesn't import from the module (which is exactly why `crime_00011` / `crime_00030` mark those tests as proving nothing). So step 0 of the refactor is **wire the tests to the real functions** — otherwise the split lands with no behavioural safety net.
- **Public surface:** keep `src/billing.ts` as a barrel re-exporting `generateInvoice`, `tax`, `recommendUpgrade`, `previewRefund`, `processRefund`. Any future external import path stays `from "./billing"`.
- **`"enterprise"` literal (`crime_00012`):** out of scope for this split, but once `types.ts` exists the `Plan` union has a home — a follow-up can replace the 4 scattered string literals (`src/api/plan.ts:2`, `src/billing.ts:7`, `src/jobs/plan-sync.ts:2`, `src/ui/pricing.ts:2`) with `Plan.Enterprise`. Don't bundle it into this PR.

### Caveat

`crime_00016` is `medium`/confidence 0.71 and the file is exactly at threshold (1.0×). On its own it would not justify a 10-file split. The reason to do it is the **stack** — `crime_00002` (high) + `crime_00009` + `crime_00013` + `crime_00017` all clear as a side effect of the seams the split creates. If `generateInvoice` weren't a God Function I'd leave the file at 310 lines.
